rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROSE PAK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Although the amended petition was uploaded and file-stamped 1/13/22, it was not actually E-filed and available for viewing until 1/20/22, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. Notice of this amended petition must be given at least 15 days before the 3/14/22 hearing. In the meantime, a supplement must be filed to explain why the attorney costs include $60 for "passport" on 11/16/18, when the accounting reflects a $100 payment for "passport" on 11/28/18. The attorney costs are not actually fully itemized, showing to whom the payments were made or what was certified, etc., however, since the dates correspond to dates shown on the register of actions, the lack of full descriptions will be overlooked in this instance. E-filing fees are just $5.00 per filing; however, in light of the attorney's cap on fees for extraordinary services, reimbursement of the full amount of costs will be allowed. The noncash figure in paragraph 20 (p. 5) is incorrect. The attorney costs in paragraph 33 (p. 13) are incorrect. (Refunds for disbursements during estate administration are not includable in the fee base. After these issues are addressed, a minor adjustment will be made to reduce the fee base by $97.54.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/4/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF LEONISA A. TRIAS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/21/22. Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. The beginning bank statement is not for the period immediately preceding the date the conservators were appointed. [Probate Code Sec. 2620(c)(2).] The attorney costs of $1,745.84 paid on 6/10/21 were not itemized. What were the "additional assets received" from the Wells Fargo account? Whose Wells Fargo account? There were credit card payments but no reported liabilities. Have all credit card bills been paid off now? There were life insurance premiums paid but no life insurance policy inventoried or on hand. The Court Investigator reported that the conservatee purchased a double burial plot in 2003, but none was inventoried or accounted for. Paragraph 12 of the petition does not indicate whether Michael Penfield meets the requirements of Bus. & Prof. Code Sec. 6450. (Most of these issues were posted in a preliminary tentative ruling on 12/23/21 but still have not been addressed as of 1/28/22.) Courtesy copies of anything filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/11/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AURA P. HUCK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although no proof of service of the NOTICE OF PETITION TO ADMINISTER ESTATE given to Axel Joachim Huck was ever filed. (Notice of Hearing for this petition or a continued hearing for this petition is NOT notice that a party is seeking appointment as a personal representative.) Counsel should cease filing her declarations when representing Court-appointed fiduciaries who are responsible for making their own declarations, not just verifying her statements. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]) No copy of the order will be returned as no SASE was provided.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/14/2022. Second continuance same issues. 1) Please file a memorandum of points and authorities explaining why a petition was filed when the Code of Civil Procedure 377.31 says, "on motion..." It appears this should be filed as a motion on the law and motion court calendar. 2) Please file an actual memorandum of points and authorities in support of the petition. 3) Notice is not proper. Please give 30 day's notice to all parties not just the attorneys in the matter. (Probate Code 1202). An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 02/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: RICHARD PATUREL REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/4/22. Issue(s) necessitating continuance: Because the conservator's first account ended on 12/31/2019 and the trustee's first account does not begin until 10/1/20, and no I&A has been filed for the trust estate, the conservator's second (final?) account must be filed so that both accountings can be reviewed together. In the meantime, this account and petition must be supplemented for the following reasons: (1) disclosures required by Probate Code Sections 1064(a)(3) and (4) are missing; (2) the date of any Court order permitting fees must be set forth; (3) incomplete descriptions of disbursements for courier services on 1/15/21, to the CPA on 12/16/20, to GIG Bookkeeping (multiple), and for "ACH Debit to Bill Pay" (multiple) must be more fully explained. "Advisory Account Credit" on 4/26/21 was presumably from Wells Fargo, but the source or payee is missing from Schedule C. Why were there multiple payments for Home Care Assistance on 4/30/21 and 6/2/21? It would be helpful if recurring disbursements showed the dates for which the services were provided. See also payments to the Caregiver without any dates or number of hours of coverage. Attorney costs advanced must be itemized. The First Republic bank statement ends on 8/10/21, not on or after 8/31/21, the ending date of the accounting period, and no reconciliation is shown for the difference between the bank statement and the balance shown in the accounting. [LRSF 14.34 D.] The filing fee for this accounting petition was $200. After the conservator's second account is filed, this petition will likely be continued further so that both can be reviewed/heard together. If the conservator's second account cannot be filed by early/mid-March, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request a continuance. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAM H. GREEN REVOCABLE TRUST TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Title To Real Property
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/14/2022. Third continuance for same issues. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1. Venue is a threshold issue that must be determined prior to the petition. Please explain and provide the legal basis /cite probate code sections for why San Francisco is the proper venue when the trustee appears to live in Edgewood, MD. 2. Please file evidence showing the Wells Fargo bank account ending in 9287 was in existence prior to execution of the trust. 3. Please file a current account statement of the bank account. 4. Please explain all the Decedent's heirs so the court can determine whether notice is proper under Prob. C. 851 (b). 5. The proposed order does not match what is requested in the prayer/request in the petition. It appears the petition must be amended to match what is requested in the proposed order as the prayer/request in the petition does not indicate the account number or where the bank account is and just says "Directing that the Bank Account is an asset of the William H. Green Revocable Trust." Also the second page of the proposed order "That the parties shall execute all documents and perform such additional acts as may be necessary to carry out the above" is not in the petition's prayer/request. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 02/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: continued to 2/28/22 (first available date). Counsel must provide a legible hard copy of the petition to the examiner in Room 202. in addition, in a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by petitioner, please address whether the order should require an accounting from the former trustee; please state the nature and value of the trust assets; and please address the qualifications and experience of the proposed succesor trustee. deadline for providing the copy and filing the supplement is noon on 2/14/22. please note, examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTELA BRAVO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. status hearing on close of estate set for 2/6/2023. Karen Ann Simons is appointed probate referee.
